FAQs/CloudAssure/AssesmentandAdvisory.title: Assesment and Advisory
FAQs/CloudAssure/AssesmentandAdvisory.description: What are Cloud Assessment Services?Cloud Assessment and Advisory is a structured engagement that thoroughly evaluates an organization’s IT infrastructure, applications, and processes to determine cloud readiness. The outcome is a tailored roadmap for migration and modernization aligned with business objectives, operational needs, and future scalability.

FAQs/CloudAssure/CloudAssure.title: Cloud Assure
FAQs/CloudAssure/CloudAssure.description: What is Cloud Assure Service (CAS)?Cloud Assure is a comprehensive cloud management service suite designed to help enterprises manage, secure, and optimize their cloud environments across public (Yntraa Cloud, AWS, Azure), private, hybrid, and multi-cloud setups. It covers the full lifecycle from assessment and migration to operations, security, optimization, and professional services.

FAQs/CloudAssure/MigrationAssist.title: Migration Assist
FAQs/CloudAssure/MigrationAssist.description: What is Cloud Migration Assist?Cloud Migration Assist is a structured service that supports all types of migrations, including on-premises to cloud, cloud-to-cloud, and legacy environment migrations. It ensures secure and efficient movement of workloads, applications, databases, and data with minimal disruption and a controlled transition.

FAQs/CloudAssure/ProfessionalService.title: Professional Service
FAQs/CloudAssure/ProfessionalService.description: What is Cloud Professional Service?Cloud Professional Service is a specialized, project-based consulting and implementation offering under the Cloud Assure portfolio. It helps organizations maximize cloud adoption, transformation, and innovation through tailored engagements spanning the entire cloud lifecycle—from strategy and adoption to optimization and continuous improvement.

FAQs/CloudAssure/ResiliencyAssuranceServ.title: Resiliency Assurance Service
FAQs/CloudAssure/ResiliencyAssuranceServ.description: What is a Disaster?Disaster is a natural or man-made (or technological) hazard resulting in an event of substantial extent causing significant physical damage or destruction or loss.

FAQs/ManagedCompute/VirtualDesktop.title: Virtual Desktop
FAQs/ManagedCompute/VirtualDesktop.description: What is Virtual Desktop-as-a-Service (vDaaS)?vDaaS is a cloud-based service that delivers secure, fully functional Windows or Linux desktops to users over the internet, supporting flexible, remote, and hybrid work across all devices. Unlike traditional PCs, these desktops are hosted in secure data centers, eliminating on-site hardware and enabling quick, scalable desktop infrastructure management.

FAQs/ManagedStorage/File Storage.title: File Storage
FAQs/ManagedStorage/File Storage.description: What is File Storage in STaaS?File Storage offers shared access using standard file protocols (NFS/SMB), suitable for collaborative workspaces, media editing, and centralized storage environments.

FAQs/Miscellaneous/FAQs.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
FAQs/Miscellaneous/FAQs.description: What happens if I miss a payment due date?Depending on how your service provider has set up the payment collections policies, one of the following might happen:

FAQs/OtherServices/ManagedDatabases.title: Managed Databases
FAQs/OtherServices/ManagedDatabases.description: What is Yntraa Cloud Managed Database as a Service (MDBaaS)?Yntraa Cloud MDBaaS is a fully managed database solution that offers enterprises scalable, secure, and high-performance databases without the need to manage the underlying infrastructure.

Knowledgebase/IPv4Networking.title: What is IPv4 Networking?
Knowledgebase/IPv4Networking.description: Internet Protocol version 4(IPv4) is the fourth generation of the Internet Protocol, and it remains the most commonly used system for assigning addresses to devices connected to a network. An IPv4 address is made up of four numbers separated by dots—like 10.0.1.15—and each address is unique, ensuring that data sent over the internet reaches the right device.

Knowledgebase/WhatisaVirtualFirewallinth.title: What is a Virtual Firewall in the Cloud?
Knowledgebase/WhatisaVirtualFirewallinth.description: A virtual firewall is a software-based tool used to protect cloud systems. Just like a physical firewall protects a building from fire, a virtual firewall helps protect your cloud network from harmful or unwanted traffic. It controls the flow of data between different parts of your system and blocks anything suspicious or risky. This keeps your applications and data safe, whether traffic is coming from outside the cloud or moving between parts of the same system.

Knowledgebase/WhatisaVPC.title: What is a VPC?
Knowledgebase/WhatisaVPC.description: A Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) is a secure, isolated section of a public cloud where you can deploy and manage your cloud resources—such as virtual machines, databases, and applications—with full control over networking and security. With a VPC, you define your own IP address ranges, create subnets, configure routing tables, and set up firewalls and gateways, allowing you to build a custom network architecture tailored to your needs.

Knowledgebase/WhatisInstances.title: What is Instances?
Knowledgebase/WhatisInstances.description: Cloud computing allows users to run virtual machines called instances, which are flexible, on-demand servers that can run different operating systems. The two most common types of instances available in cloud environments are Linux instances and Windows instances. Both serve unique purposes and cater to different application needs.

Knowledgebase/WhatisIPAddressSubnetTiera.title: What is IP Address, Subnet, Tier and CIDR?
Knowledgebase/WhatisIPAddressSubnetTiera.description: Every device or service in a network—whether it's a virtual machine, load balancer, or database—needs a unique IP address to talk to other devices. It’s like a digital address for communication. For example, a VM may be assigned 192.168.1.10, which allows it to send and receive data across the network.

ReleaseNotes/Ver1000.title: Version 1.0.0.0
ReleaseNotes/Ver1000.description: We are excited to announce the general availability of Yntraa Cloud Platform, a modern cloud infrastructure platform designed to support enterprise applications, data platforms, and digital services.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/AboutYntraaAcc.title: About Yntraa Account Centre
Subscribers/AccountCentre/AboutYntraaAcc.description: The Account Centre on the Yntraa Cloud is a non-technical (commercial and support) dashboard used for viewing and managing all non-technical aspects of your Yntraa cloud account. You can access the Account Centre by clicking on any item in the user menu at the top-right, the help/support icon in the header, or the wallet icon in the header.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/InvoicesandCre.title: Invoices and Credit Notes
Subscribers/AccountCentre/InvoicesandCre.description: Invoices are generated at the end of each billing cycle for the services consumed during the billing cycle. Invoices can be accessed from the Billing > Invoices & Credit Notes section on the main navigation panel.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/Organisation-A.title: Organisation/Account Profile
Subscribers/AccountCentre/Organisation-A.description: Using the Profile section, you can manage your organisation account profile on Yntraa Cloud. The profile management section can be accessed from the Account Centre in the top helper bar by clicking on the organisation. Account profiles include the following:

Subscribers/AccountCentre/Payments.title: Payments
Subscribers/AccountCentre/Payments.description: Yntraa Cloud supports three ways of making payments or adding money to the wallet:

Subscribers/AccountCentre/ResettingPassw.title: Resetting Password
Subscribers/AccountCentre/ResettingPassw.description: To reset your One Yotta account password, follow these step:

Subscribers/AccountCentre/StandardAgreem.title: Standard Agreements
Subscribers/AccountCentre/StandardAgreem.description: Standard agreements and documents are available on the Yntraa Cloud Account Centre. Whenever any of these documents is amended or updated, you will be prompted to accept the modified documents on your next login.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/Subscriptionsa.title: Subscriptions and Services
Subscribers/AccountCentre/Subscriptionsa.description: A subscription is a record created whenever a new resource is purchased or a service is activated. In other words, a subscription is a financial ‘agreement’ that links the item that has been purchased, its value, and its renewal frequency.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/SupportandTick.title: Support and Tickets
Subscribers/AccountCentre/SupportandTick.description: Yntraa Cloud provides SLA-driven support to all subscriber customers in accordance with the terms and conditions outlined in the Service Level Agreement. To access the SLA, navigate to the User icon in the top helper bar and select Account under Account Centre.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/TeamMemberMana.title: Team Member Management
Subscribers/AccountCentre/TeamMemberMana.description: Team members can be onboarded using the Team section from the Account Centre, allows you to add billing, technical, or other admin users who can log in to your account and perform operations.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/UnbilledCharge.title: Unbilled Charges
Subscribers/AccountCentre/UnbilledCharge.description: All subscriptions incur prorated charges during an account’s billing cycle. These charges, also known as unbilled charges, can be accessed from Billing > Unbilled Charges in the main navigation panel.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/WalletandTrans.title: Wallet and Vouchers
Subscribers/AccountCentre/WalletandTrans.description: For all practical purposes, the service balance acts as your wallet balance or purchase capacity at any given point of time. The service balance is an aggregation of your credit limit, total payments made, and the total charges incurred on your account. Your service balance is always displayed on the top bar and gets updated in real-time when a transaction or a charge is recorded.

Subscribers/AdvanceServices/DevOpsasaSer.title: DevOps as a Service
Subscribers/AdvanceServices/DevOpsasaSer.description: To activate the desired DevOps service, perform the following steps:

Subscribers/CloudAssure/AssessmentandAdv.title: Assessment and Advisory
Subscribers/CloudAssure/AssessmentandAdv.description: Cloud Assessment and Advisory Service helps organizations assess their current IT landscape and define a clear, strategic roadmap for cloud adoption. It provides expert guidance and tailored recommendations to ensure a secure, cost-effective, and well-aligned cloud transformation.

Subscribers/CloudAssure/MigrationAssist.title: Migration Assist
Subscribers/CloudAssure/MigrationAssist.description: Cloud Migration Service enables the secure and seamless transition of applications, data, and infrastructure to cloud or hybrid environments. Through a structured approach and tailored migration strategies, it minimizes risk and disruption while accelerating the benefits of cloud adoption.

Subscribers/CloudAssure/MonitoringandNot.title: Monitoring and Notifications
Subscribers/CloudAssure/MonitoringandNot.description: Cloud Monitoring and Notification Service ensures continuous visibility into the health and performance of cloud resources through real-time monitoring, centralized logging, and automated alerts. It enables timely issue detection and response, helping maintain uptime,

Subscribers/CloudAssure/OptimisationServ.title: Optimisation Service
Subscribers/CloudAssure/OptimisationServ.description: Cloud Optimisation Service enhances cost and performance efficiency across hybrid and multi-cloud environments through a structured, FinOps-aligned approach. It identifies optimisation opportunities, strengthens cost governance, and enables organisations to reduce waste while maximizing the value of their cloud investments.

Subscribers/CloudAssure/ProfessionalServ.title: Professional Services
Subscribers/CloudAssure/ProfessionalServ.description: Cloud Professional Services provide structured, expert-led support to help organizations plan, implement, and optimize their cloud environments. These services cover the complete cloud lifecycle, including strategy, migration, modernization, and ongoing improvement.

Subscribers/CloudAssure/ResiliencyAssura.title: Resiliency Assurance Service
Subscribers/CloudAssure/ResiliencyAssura.description: Resiliency Assurance Service is a disaster recovery, focused solution designed to help organizations quickly restore critical infrastructure, applications, and data after disruptions.

Subscribers/CloudAssure/SecurityandCompl.title: Security and Compliance
Subscribers/CloudAssure/SecurityandCompl.description: To activate the desired security and compliance service, perform the following steps:

Subscribers/Compute/Autoscale/AboutAutos.title: About Autoscale
Subscribers/Compute/Autoscale/AboutAutos.description: The Autoscale feature allows a system to adjust its computing resources based on the changing workload automatically. In instances, autoscaling helps ensure that an application or service has the right amount of resources to handle varying levels of demand efficiently. The key concept behind autoscaling is dynamic resource allocation. When demand for a particular application or service increases, autoscaling automatically adds more computing resources (such as instances) to handle the load. Conversely, during periods of lower demand, autoscaling reduces the number of resources to optimize cost and efficiency.

Subscribers/Compute/Autoscale/CreatingAu.description: To create Autoscale Groups, the following steps are:

Subscribers/Compute/Autoscale/Requiremen.title: Requirements for Autoscale
Subscribers/Compute/Autoscale/Requiremen.description: - Yntraa Cloud now uses the native Autoscale feature of ACS. This feature is available with ACS version 4.18.0.0 onwards.

Subscribers/Compute/Kubernetes/AboutKube.title: About Kubernetes
Subscribers/Compute/Kubernetes/AboutKube.description: Yntraa Cloud users the ability to deploy highly-available Kubernetes clusters for their cloud-native development, deployment and DevOps use cases. The Yntraa Kubernetes Service (YK8s) extends basic and advanced cluster management functionalities via the cloud console UI and the kubectl utility.

Subscribers/Compute/Kubernetes/AboutKube.title: About Kubernetes Dashboard
Subscribers/Compute/Kubernetes/AboutKube.description: The Kubernetes Dashboard is a web-based user interface that provides a visual representation of a cluster's resources and enables easier management and monitoring. Here's an overview of the dashboard:

Subscribers/Compute/Kubernetes/Accessing.title: Accessing a Cluster using the Command Line
Subscribers/Compute/Kubernetes/Accessing.description: Kubernetes clusters can be accessed and controlled from the command line using the kubeconfig manifest for the cluster. Each cluster has a unique manifest which is required to identify and target the cluster using the kubectl utility.

Subscribers/Compute/Kubernetes/Accessing.description: To access the dashboard in Kubernetes version 1.24 and onwards, the following steps are:

Subscribers/Compute/Kubernetes/ClusterOp.description: Kubernetes clusters created on Yntraa Cloud allow for a set of management operations from the cloud console UI. While these options can be controlled using kubectl, these are provided on the UI for quick and easy access.

Subscribers/Compute/Kubernetes/IngressNe.description: To view networking, navigate to Kubernetes and select a cluster details. This section manage a cluster's firewalls and ingress networking. Here, Port forwarding and load balancing rules can also be managed using kubectl.

Subscribers/Compute/Kubernetes/Overview.title: Overview
Subscribers/Compute/Kubernetes/Overview.description: To view the below details, navigate to Kubernetes, select a Kubernetes Cluster, and access the Overview tab.

Subscribers/Compute/Kubernetes/ScalingKu.title: Scaling Kubernetes Clusters
Subscribers/Compute/Kubernetes/ScalingKu.description: Yntraa Cloud allows for manual and automatic cluster scaling. Clusters scaling can be configured from the Nodes section of cluster details or from the Operations section of cluster details.

Subscribers/Compute/Kubernetes/ViewingKu.title: Viewing Kubernetes Cluster Details
Subscribers/Compute/Kubernetes/ViewingKu.description: Yntraa Cloud offers a detailed view of Kubernetes clusters on the UI. Yntraa also brings the full power and accessibility of cluster management via the kubectl interface.

Subscribers/Compute/LinuxInstances/About.description: Yntraa Cloud offers a highly usable and visual way of working with and operating Linux Instances using the cloud console. You can access all the Linux Instances available in your account in the following way:

Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/AboutR.title: About RHEL Instances
Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/AboutR.description: Instances are computing units that provide you with resources to run your applications/workloads. Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) Instances are virtual machines that run the images of RHEL Operating System.

Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/Config.title: Configuring Alerts
Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/Config.description: Alerts get triggered whenever a configured condition is met. You can create multiple alerts on an instance. Alerts are sent to recipients that you can define and manage.

Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/Connec.description: You can connect to RHEL Instances via SSH sessions using passwords (less secure) or SSH key pairs (most secure).

Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Abo.description: Instances are computing units that provide you with resources to run your applications/workloads. Windows Instances are virtual machines that run the images of Windows Operating Systems.

Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Con.description: To view the configured alerts or configure new ones, navigate to Operating Windows Instances, select a Windows Instance and access the Alerts tab .

Subscribers/GettingStartedonYntraaCloud.title: Getting Started
Subscribers/GettingStartedonYntraaCloud.description: After completing the sign-up process, the user receives an email with the details to access the Cloud Console.

Subscribers/intro.title: Introduction
Subscribers/intro.description: Yntraa Cloud is a powerful, all-in-one solution designed to make managing your cloud and network services simple and efficient. Whether you are a business, an organization, or an individual, Yntraa provides everything you need to keep your digital infrastructure running smoothly.

Subscribers/ManagedCompute/BareMetal.title: Bare Metal
Subscribers/ManagedCompute/BareMetal.description: Bare Metal Service provides direct access to dedicated physical servers without virtualization overhead, ensuring optimal performance, low latency, and predictable throughput for performance-critical workloads.

Subscribers/ManagedCompute/ContainerRegi.title: Container Registry
Subscribers/ManagedCompute/ContainerRegi.description: To activate the desired Container Registry service, perform the following steps:

Subscribers/ManagedCompute/VirtualDeskto.title: Virtual Desktops
Subscribers/ManagedCompute/VirtualDeskto.description: Virtual Desktop is a cloud-delivered desktop solution that enables secure access to applications and workspaces from any device, anywhere. Designed for modern hybrid work environments, it offers centralized management, enterprise-grade security, and scalable performance, helping organizations enhance productivity while optimizing IT costs.

Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/Backuphighav.title: Backup, High Availability and Disaster Recovery

Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/introduction.title: Introduction
Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/introduction.description: Yntraa Cloud Managed Database as a Service (MDBaaS) is a fully managed database offering that enables you to deploy, operate, and scale databases on Yntraa Cloud without managing the underlying infrastructure or database operations.

Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/MDBaas.title: Managed Databases Offering
Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/MDBaas.description: In this section, the following Databases are provided:

Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/Postprovisio.title: Post-Provisioning Access and Visibility
Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/Postprovisio.description: After a Managed Database as a Service (MDBaaS) instance is successfully provisioned, customers are provided with database-level access and connection details required for application integration.

Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/PreRequisite.title: Pre-Requisites
Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/PreRequisite.description: Before initiating a Managed Database as a Service (MDBaaS) request on Yntraa Cloud, you must ensure that the following prerequisites are met.

Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/Scalingandco.title: Scaling and Configuration Management
Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/Scalingandco.description: Yntraa Cloud Managed Database as a Service (MDBaaS) supports controlled scaling and configuration changes to ensure platform stability and predictable performance.

Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/servicedepro.title: Service Deprovisioning
Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/servicedepro.description: Service de-provisioning refers to the permanent termination of a Managed Database as a Service (MDBaaS) instance on Yntraa Cloud.

Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/Serviceoverv.title: Service Overview and Architecture
Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/Serviceoverv.description: Yntraa Cloud Managed Database as a Service (MDBaaS) provides a standardized, managed deployment of databases on Yntraa Cloud infrastructure using a QuickPlan-based provisioning model, where:

Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/Serviceprovi.title: Service Provisioning
Subscribers/ManagedDatabase/Serviceprovi.description: Managed Databases is a cloud-based service that takes care of database setup, maintenance, scaling, and security, so organizations do not have to manage it themselves. It supports both relational and non-relational databases, ensuring high performance, availability, and reliability.

Subscribers/ManagedNetwork/LoadBalancer.title: Load Balancer
Subscribers/ManagedNetwork/LoadBalancer.description: Load Balancer is a virtual solution that spreads incoming traffic across multiple servers to maintain high performance, reliability, and smooth user experience. It uses configurable algorithms to distribute requests efficiently, preventing server overload and ensuring better application availability.

Subscribers/ManagedSecurity/EndPointSecu.title: End Point Security
Subscribers/ManagedSecurity/EndPointSecu.description: Endpoint Security protects end-user devices such as desktops, laptops, and mobile phones from malicious and unauthorized activities through advanced security practices and technologies. It safeguards corporate networks by securing the devices employees use to access applications, systems, and data, preventing unauthorised access, data breaches, and cyber threats across the organisation.

Subscribers/ManagedSecurity/YottaSmartCS.title: Yotta Smart CSOC
Subscribers/ManagedSecurity/YottaSmartCS.description: Yotta Smart Complementary Subservice Organization Controls (CSOC) is a security operations service designed to help organizations effectively manage and respond to growing and complex cyber threats. It combines advanced monitoring and automated response capabilities with expert support to detect, investigate, and remediate security incidents quickly.

Subscribers/Networking/IPv4-basedNetwork.title: About IPv4-based Networking
Subscribers/Networking/IPv4-basedNetwork.description: IPv4 Addresses form the backbone of networking operations on Yntraa. Internally, IPv4 Addresses are treated as a monetisable Service on Yntraa. However, the use of IPv4 Addresses is implicit as part of other essential Services.

Subscribers/Networking/IPv4-basedNetwork.title: Establishing Site-to-Site VPN Between Two VPCs
Subscribers/Networking/IPv4-basedNetwork.description: A Site-to-Site Virtual Private Network (VPN) provides a secure and encrypted connection between two Virtual Private Clouds (VPC), enabling seamless communication across geographically separated networks. This setup is commonly used to connect different environments (such as production and development) or extend on-premises infrastructure to the cloud.

Subscribers/Networking/IPv4-basedNetwork.title: Managing VPN Gateways and Site-to-Site VPN
Using Remote Access VPN with VPC
Subscribers/Networking/IPv4-basedNetwork.description: To use a VPN client to connect to your VPC, follow these steps:

Subscribers/Networking/L2 Networks/About.title: About L2 Networks
Subscribers/Networking/L2 Networks/About.description: L2 networks, often called data link layer networks, play a crucial role in computer networking by establishing direct connections between devices in a local network and ensuring data packets are delivered accurately to their intended destination.

Subscribers/Networking/L2 Networks/AddNe.title: Adding New L2 Network
Subscribers/Networking/L2 Networks/AddNe.description: To add a new L2 network, click the New Network button. The following screen appears:

Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/About.title: About NAT Gateways
Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/About.description: A Network Address Translation (NAT) gateway is a networking component in cloud and on-premises environments that enables resources within a private subnet (such as virtual machines or containers) to connect to the internet or other external networks, without exposing these resources directly to incoming connections from those external networks. This helps in enhancing security in cloud infrastructure by keeping resources in private subnets isolated from direct public access.

Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/Manag.title: Instances
Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/Manag.description: This section lists all the instances that are a part of this Virtual Firewall.

Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/Manag.title: IPv4 Addresses
Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/Manag.description: Each virtual gateway comes with a default IPv4 address that belongs to the Virtual Gateway governing the network. The default (source - NAT) IPv4 address can be used to access the Virtual Gateway publicly or via Remote Access (RA) VPN.

Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/Manag.title: Operations
Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/Manag.description: You can perform the following operations in the Operations section:

Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/Manag.title: Overview
Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/Manag.description: Navigate to the Overview tab to view the following details:

Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/Manag.title: Viewing and Managing NAT Gateways
Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/Manag.description: To manage a NAT gateway, follow these steps:

Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/PortF.title: Port Forwarding for VM via VNF
Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/PortF.description: This section outlines the steps to configure port forwarding for a Virtual Machine (VM) using a Virtual Network Function (VNF) in a cloud environment. Port forwarding allows external clients to securely access services (for example SSH, HTTP) hosted on the VM by mapping ports from the VNF’s public IP to the VM’s private IP. This approach improves network segmentation and control by routing all incoming traffic through the VNF layer.

Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/PortF.title: Port Forwarding with Public IP
Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/PortF.description: In Yntraa Cloud, an additional Public IP enables you to expose internal services hosted on virtual machines (VMs) to the internet by creating dedicated port forwarding rules. This approach is especially useful when multiple external IPs are needed for services such as SSH, HTTP, or custom applications. By assigning specific public IPs to particular services or VMs, you can achieve greater control, flexibility, and network segmentation.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/A.title: Accessing the VFI Control Panel
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/A.description: The Virtual Firewall Service is delivered as an integration using OS images pre-loaded with pfSense or FortiGate VM.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/A.title: Adding Instances to a VFI-based Network
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/A.description: Instances can be created and placed in the L2-LAN (Free Networks) created by the Virtual Firewall. These Instances use special Linux OS images, which are available as a separate OS Collection. Users can create Linux Instances, and choose the appropriate options/controls to create an Instance in the Virtual Firewall network.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/C.title: Creating a Virtual Firewall
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/C.description: To create a Virtual Firewall, follow these steps:

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/D.title: Deploying VNF and VM Within VNF
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/D.description: This section provides a step-by-step guide for deploying a Virtual Network Function (VNF)—specifically a virtual firewall—and provisioning a Virtual Machine (VM) behind it using the Yntraa Cloud. This setup allows users to route VM traffic through the VNF, enabling secure and segmented network environments.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.title: About Firewall Instances
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.description: Virtual Firewalls enables you to use Virtual Firewalls in a Virtualized Network Function (VNF) style, which include support for multiple VLANs and Public IPv4 addresses and automated service activation. This allows for more robust firewall management capabilities.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.title: Configuring Alerts
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.description: To view the configured alerts or configure new ones, navigate to the Virtual Firewall and access the Alerts tab.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.title: Networking Management
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.description: To view the networks that of particular Instance, navigate to the Virtual Firewall and access the Networking tab.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.title: Operations
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.description: To view all available Instance operations, navigate to the Networking, select a Virtual Firewall and access the Operations tab.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.title: Overview
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.description: To view the below details, navigate to the Firewall Security, select the Virtual Firewall and access the Overview tab.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.title: Reconfiguring Virtual Firewall
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.description: To view available reconfiguration options, navigate to the Networking section and select a Virtual Firewall and access the Reconfigure tab.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.title: Restore Points
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.description: To view all the restore points taken for Instance, navigate to the Networking, select a Virtual Firewall and access the Restore Points tab.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.title: Viewing Graphs and Utilization
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.description: To view the available graphs and monitor the instance in real-time, navigate to the Virtual Firewall and access the Graphs tab.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.title: Viewing Virtual Firewall Details
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.description: As the virtual firewalls are created, Cloud Console users can view the enabled Virtual Firewalls in the Networking > Virtual Firewalls section. The following details are shown:

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.title: Volume Management
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.description: To view the disks attached to this Instance, navigate to the Networking, select a Virtual Firewall and access the Volumes tab.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.title: About Virtual Private Clouds
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.description: Details about Virtual Private Cloud operations and actions can be found in their respective sections.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.title: Creating VPC Subnets and Tiers
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.description: VPCs follow the convention of 3-tiered architectures, with web, app, and DB tiers forming the norm. You can, however, configure these tiers to suit your application architecture or just follow the common convention.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.title: IPv4 Addresses and VPC
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.description: IPv4 Addresses are an integral part of using VPC networking, and need to be used to access various components of the VPC. By default, a public IPv4 Address is assigned to the VR which can communicate through the internet to transmit traffic to/from the VR. This IPv4 can also be used for configuring remote access (L2TP) and site-to-site (IPSec) VPN connections.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.title: Managing Access Control on VPC Subnets
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.description: Access control policies can be created using Access Control Lists (ACL) and configuring rules within these ACL (called ACL Rules). An ACL can then be applied to any tier within the VPC. These policies govern what traffic is allowed or restricted into and from your VPC network tiers.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.title: Overview
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.description: To view a list of sections and the various operations or actions you can perform by going inside the particular section, click on the VPC name. Below the VPC name is an informational view where you can find the details below.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.title: Reconfiguring a VPC
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.description: The Reconfigure section displays the current subscription details and allows modification of the networking pack under the monthly pricing plan.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.title: VPC Management and Basic Operations
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.description: VPC management offers the following operations. These are basic VPC management actions and don't have any impact on the actual network configurations.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.title: Difference Between L2 Networks and VPC
Working with VPN Connections in a VPC
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.description: The VPC feature on Yntraa provides advanced networking capabilities for use with VPN gateways and connections over an IPSec tunnel. Yntraa gives the ability to create virtual private networks (VPN) to access virtual machines inside a VPC.

Subscribers/OtherServices/CDNasaService.title: CDN as a Service
Subscribers/OtherServices/CDNasaService.description: A Content Delivery Network (CDN) is a system that helps websites load faster for users. It stores copies of website content on multiple servers in different locations and delivers it from the nearest server to the user. This reduces loading time, improves performance, and ensures a smooth browsing experience.

Subscribers/OtherServices/CyberSecurityP.title: Cyber Security Professional Services
Subscribers/OtherServices/CyberSecurityP.description: Cyber Security Professional Services help organisations protect their systems, applications, and data from cyber threats by assessing risks, identifying vulnerabilities, and implementing effective security controls.

Subscribers/OtherServices/DDosProtection.title: DDoS Protection
Subscribers/OtherServices/DDosProtection.description: Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attack is a cyberattack that disrupts a website, server, or network by overwhelming it with excessive traffic from multiple compromised systems. This makes services unavailable to legitimate users and can severely impact business operations.

Subscribers/OtherServices/GlobalCloudCon.title: Global Cloud Connect
Subscribers/OtherServices/GlobalCloudCon.description: Yntraa Global Cloud Connect, provides a secure and high-speed private connection between on-premise environments and major cloud platforms such as Microsoft Azure, AWS, Google Cloud, and Oracle Cloud. By avoiding the public internet, it reduces network delays, improves reliability, and allows businesses to easily connect and manage multiple cloud platforms through a single secure link.

Subscribers/OtherServices/HostedInternet.title: Hosted Internet Bandwidth
Subscribers/OtherServices/HostedInternet.description: Yotta’s Hosted Internet Bandwidth service delivers high-speed, reliable, and scalable internet connectivity from its carrier-neutral data centers. With multiple telecom providers, diverse fiber paths, and built-in redundancy, it ensures maximum uptime and seamless performance.

Subscribers/OtherServices/HSMandKMS.title: HSM and KMS
Subscribers/OtherServices/HSMandKMS.description: In cloud services, Hardware Security Module (HSM) and Key Management Service (KMS) work together to protect sensitive data by securely creating, storing, and managing encryption keys.

Subscribers/OtherServices/LoadTestingasa.title: Load Testing as a Service
Subscribers/OtherServices/LoadTestingasa.description: In cloud services, Load Testing as a Service (LTaaS) helps organizations evaluate the performance, scalability, and stability of applications by simulating real-world user traffic and workloads. It enables teams to identify performance bottlenecks, measure system behavior under different load conditions, and ensure that applications can handle peak demand efficiently, thereby improving reliability, user experience, and overall application performance in cloud environments.

Subscribers/OtherServices/MessagingServi.title: Messaging Services
Subscribers/OtherServices/MessagingServi.description: In cloud services, Messaging Service enables organisations to build event-driven and real-time applications using managed messaging platforms like RabbitMQ and Kafka. It provides reliable queues and event streams without the complexity of managing infrastructure, ensuring high availability, security, and performance while teams focus on application development.

Subscribers/OtherServices/MultiTenantBra.title: Multi-Tenant BraaS
Subscribers/OtherServices/MultiTenantBra.description: Multi-tenant Backup, Recovery, and Archival as a Service (BRaaS) is a fully managed solution that protects and restores business data across physical, virtual, and cloud environments. It helps organizations stay prepared for data loss, cyber threats, or system failures by ensuring secure backups and quick recovery.

Subscribers/OtherServices/PrivilegedAcce.title: Privileged Access Management
Subscribers/OtherServices/PrivilegedAcce.description: In modern IT environments, Privileged Access Management (PAM) protects critical systems and sensitive data by securely managing and controlling privileged accounts. While PAM safeguards privileged credentials and enforces strict access controls, it also monitors privileged sessions and activities to prevent misuse or unauthorized access, ensuring improved security, visibility, and control across on-premises and cloud environments.

Subscribers/OtherServices/SecurityTestin.title: Security Testing
Subscribers/OtherServices/SecurityTestin.description: Security Testing helps identify vulnerabilities and security weaknesses in applications, systems, and networks. It uses methods such as Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing (VAPT) to detect potential risks and improve overall security.

Subscribers/OtherServices/SIEM.title: SIEM
Subscribers/OtherServices/SIEM.description: Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) in cloud service is a centralised security solution that collects and analyzes data from multiple sources to detect and respond to threats in real time. It provides log management, monitoring, alerts, and compliance reporting, helping organizations improve visibility and strengthen their overall security posture.

Subscribers/OtherServices/UTMasaService.title: UTM as a Service
Subscribers/OtherServices/UTMasaService.description: Unified Threat Management (UTM) in cloud service is a centralised security solution that combines firewall, antivirus, intrusion prevention, and content filtering into a single platform. It simplifies security management while providing comprehensive protection against cyber threats.

Subscribers/OtherServices/WAFasaService.title: WAF as a Service
Subscribers/OtherServices/WAFasaService.description: A Web Application Firewall (WAF) is a security solution that protects web applications by monitoring and filtering incoming and outgoing traffic. It inspects data at the application layer to detect and block malicious or suspicious activity, ensuring secure and reliable web performance.

Subscribers/SigningIn.title: Signing In
Subscribers/SigningIn.description: To Sign In to your Yntraa Cloud, follow these steps:

Subscribers/SigningUp.title: Signing Up
Subscribers/SigningUp.description: To get started with the Yntraa Cloud, you must create an account on the One Yotta account.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/AboutBl.title: About Block Volumes
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/AboutBl.description: Yntraa Block Volumes are the underlying software-defined volume management layer that powers various storage-related or storage-driven cloud services. Block Volumes can be used with Linux and Windows instances as root and data disks, with Kubernetes as persistent storage, or simply as a storehouse for snapshots and images.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Creatin.title: Creating Data Disk
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Creatin.description: To create a Block Volume data disk, follow the below steps:

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.title: Operations
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.description: To view all available Block Volume operations, navigate to the Storage, select the Block Volumes and access the Operations tab. The following window appears:

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.title: Overview
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.description: Navigate to the Overview tab to view the following details:

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.title: Resize the Block Volume
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.description: To resize an available Block Volumes, navigate to Storage, select the Block Volumes and access the Resize tab.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.title: Creating Automated Restore Point Schedules
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.description: Yntraa Cloud users can create restore point policies for their root and data disks. These schedules enable for strategic management of data retention policies and can be used as an alternative backup solution. To create or manage the restore point schedules, navigate to the Schedules section of disk details.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.title: Working with Disk Restore Points
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.description: Yntraa block volumes service provides extensive functionality for managing disk restore points. Restore points are point-in-time 'images' of a disk contents and can be used as a restoration point for the parent disk. The following sections outline all available restore points functions and capabilities in the Yntraa Cloud.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/UsingBl.title: Using Block Volumes with Kubernetes
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/UsingBl.description: Yntraa Kubernetes also uses the underlying Block Volumes service to create persistent volumes for Kubernetes clusters. Yntraa Cloud users can specify the root disk size for each cluster node (control and worker), and the Block Volume service creates the nodes in the cluster with a root disk of the specified size.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Working.title: Working with Instance Volumes
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Working.description: Each Instance on Yntraa Cloud (Linux or Windows) includes a 'root' disk or volume. This is the primary disk partition on the Instance, where the operating system, data, components and files reside on the root disk.

Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorage/AboutY.title: About Yntraa Cloud Object Storage
Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorage/AboutY.description: Yntraa Cloud Object Storage provides scalable and flexible storage for unstructured data in the form of objects. In object storage, data is stored as discrete objects containing data, metadata, and a unique identifier. Yntraa Cloud delivers the service through an integration with Object Storage Platform.

Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorage/Creati.title: Creating Object Storage
Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorage/Creati.description: An Object Storage  is where you store your files in the cloud. Creating an Object Storage is the first step to organize, access, and manage your data easily.

Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorage/Featur.title: Feature List and Integration Details
Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorage/Featur.description: Yntraa Storage is delivered as an integration with a leading object storage platform. The integration functions using API-driven checkpoints.

Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorage/Freque.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorage/Freque.description: What is object storage in the context of Yntraa Cloud?object storage is a scalable and durable storage solution for managing unstructured data such as files, images, videos, and backups.

Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/AboutTools.title: About Tools and Utilities
Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/AboutTools.description: The Tools and Utilities sections in the Yntraa Cloud Console keep a record of cloud management utilities and assets that might be needed to use specific sections of the Yntraa powered cloud.

Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/AddingVPNG.title: Adding VPN Gateways
Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/AddingVPNG.description: VPN Gateways allow you to establish secure connections between networks and cloud resources. The Yntraa Cloud Console enables you to create and manage VPN Gateways to ensure safe and reliable communication using encrypted tunnels over the internet.

Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingCu.title: Managing Custom Templates and Images
Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingCu.description: Custom OS templates can be created from a Restore Point. To view custom templates, navigate to Tools and Utilities > My Images. The following details are displayed:

Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingIn.title: Managing Instance and Disk Restore Points
Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingIn.description: Restore points use the Yntraa Block Volumes service and occupy billable storage space.

Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingSS.title: Managing SSH Keys and Key Pairs for Linux Instances
Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingSS.description: The Yntraa Cloud Console allows you to create secure shell connections to Instances in your account by using SSH keys, in addition to using Instance passwords. Since passwords can be easily compromised, SSH keys provide a more secure alternative. These keys function as encrypted signatures and work only when their public and private components match.
